body:
Oh god where to start. Off the top of my head;

- A lack of critical thinking. Everything is an extreme - there is no nuance. You are either 110% is favor or something, or you're the most drastic flavor of evil. There is nothing in-between. People are varied, of course they are going to hold differing opinions. But abusing them and shouting them down doesn't help change opinions or further discussion, it just drives people away. So we become an echo chamber.

- Short comments are dominating in a way they didn't as much, historically. I blame mobile-apps.

- We've a strange amount of users, new and old, who will just casually ask people to off themselves or say someone should be hanged/shot or otherwise killed.

- YouTubers think we're the best place ever ever to launch their content. So now everything from there is viewed with mire. 

- We're a placesub so should have a wide variety of content, but our users are obsessed with politics, despite there being a sub specifically for it. And their political slant is similar and reinforced by the hivemind. So any counterpoint, no matter how faithfully engaged or articulated, is attacked. Though this problem is prevalent throughout Reddit, and is why splintersubs form. I blame the voting system.

- Traveller hate is just, unfathomably prevalent and absolutely awful beyond any other topic we endure.

- Agenda-commenters target us with a special level of vigor. Especially on hotbed issues relating to veganism, zionism, trans, etc. (Our weirdest one is some sort of Margaret Thatcher defence obsessive).

- We are targeted by issue subs, meta subs, discord groups, and other offsite org.

- All our 'discussion' attracts Reddit obsessives - those who make new accounts after being banned, purchase used accounts to avoid standard tool detection, etc. Or even just long-time users which appear on the same topic over and over with a somewhat uneasy perspective, but without breaking rules. It's one thing to do this when you just want to post pictures of pasties. It's quite another when one is trying to further their point that some politician is some sort of Nazi sympathiser or beholden to some nationstate *sigh*.

- Agenda-posters make up the majority of our subs current-events submissions. And it usually has some sort of political slant.

- Due to the paywall nature of many publications, we lack cross-spectrum content. We're pretty much a mirror for BBC News and The Guardian.

Good god, the STEM Lordiness here is insufferable. Get your head out of your compiler. You cannot program a society; you cannot program people; you cannot program the laws that govern either. A compiler-ready code of law would be unworkable in the fuzzy illogic of the real word. Lawyers are lying pieces of shit and we should hold assumptions of eliteness at the USSC level at bay, but the point here is that they are skilled in using valid logic and/or illogic to swindle their way through hearings without lying. That requires a high level of critical thinking and problem solving ability in their domain. Their domain is not programming; it's nothing less than trying to get noncompliant, illogical actors (society) to conform to a set of rules, none of which have to be logical because this is the real non-boolean world. Ffs, reddit.
